original_hypothesis.title: Setting taxCut to Zero Bricks Protocol Operations
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: If the owner sets taxCut to 0, any function that distributes fees (minting, forging, trading) will revert due to division by zero in NukeFund.receive or EntityForging.forgeWithListed.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While this requires admin action, the impact is a complete halt of all economic activity in the protocol.

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: No validation preventing taxCut from being 0

analysis_questions: Are there any safeguards preventing taxCut from being set to 0?
validation_requirements: Call setTaxCut(0) and attempt to mint or forge

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: The admin accidentally sets taxCut to 0. All subsequent mints, trades, and forges revert, completely bricking the protocol until the admin corrects the value.
prerequisites: Admin error or compromised admin key

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Temporary halt of all protocol revenue and operations
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Admin can set taxCut back to > 0

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
